ab: maqati aken a kisedjam tua su ginpiya?
en: A: Can I borrow money from you?

ab: maqati! pida su kinasaljinga?
en: B: Yes! How much do you need?

ab: namadraudraw aken a masi ginpiya, maqadi aken a kisedjam tjanusun?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: inika ken a nakemacu tua ginpiya uta!
en: B: I don’t have any money either!

ab: namadraudraw aken a kemacu tua ginpiya, maqadi aken a kisedjam tjanusun?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: ini, katjaisangas a su kinisedjam tjanuaken a ginpiya inianan ka su pacikeli.
ch: B:不要，上次借的錢你都還沒還我。
en: B: No, you haven’t paid me back the money you borrowed last time.

ab: maqati aken a kisedjam tua su kava?
en: A: Can I borrow your clothes?

ab: nanguaq, alapu a kisumadju.
en: B: Well, you take it yourself.

ab: maqadi aken a kisedjam tua su sapitj?
en: A: Can I borrow your book?

ab: ku sinipasedjam anga tjai Tjinuay.
en: B: I lent it to Tjinuay.

ab: pasedjaman tjanuaken a su zidinsia uii?
ch: A:請借給我你的腳踏車好嗎?
en: A: Can I borrow your bicycle, please?

ab: uii, ljakua pacikelu nutiyaw tjanuaken.
ch: B: 好，但是明天要還給我。
en: B: Okay, but you have to give it back to me tomorrow.

ab: qemudjalj anga, inika ken a nakemacu tua kausua, maqati aken a su pasedjaman?
ch: A:下雨了，我沒有帶傘，借我傘好嗎?
en: A: It’s raining, I don’t have an umbrella, can you lend me one?

ab: ita a ku kausua, pai kadjadjalain a kiljaung!
ch: B: 我只有一把傘，我們一起撐吧!
en: B: I only have an umbrella, let’s stick together!

ab: aicu a ku kinisedjam tjanusun ka tjaisangas a sapitj, ku pacikelaw tjanusun.
ch: A:這是你上次借我的書，還給你。
en: A: This is the book you borrowed from me last time, I give it back to you.

ab: nu sapacun anan sun ta anema a sapitj pasemalavi anga ken.
ch: B:還想看什麼書都可以跟我說。
en: B:You can tell me what else you want to read.

ab: numakudakuda a uri ku sipacikelan ta kinpiya tjanusun?
en: A: When do I have to pay you back?

ab: iniamakuda, nuizuanga su kimpiya qaqivuanga.
ch: B:沒關係，你有錢的時候再說。
en: B: It doesn’t matter, you can talk about it when you have money.

ab: nungida uri semukiyam sun tjanuaken?
en: B: When are you going to pay me back?

ab: ku su kiamai anga sun nutiyaw.
en: A: I will return it to you tomorrow.

ab: nungida anga uri su pacikelen aku sapitj?
en: B: When are you going to return my book?

ab: matu ku pinacikel anga ka tiyaw?
ch: B:我昨天不是已經還你了嗎?
en: A: Didn’t I return it to you yesterday?

ab: aicu a kinpic sinipasedjam ni Kuliw tjanusun?
en: A: Did Kuliw lend you this pen?

ab: uii, ku kinisedjam tjaimadju tucu a qadaw.
en: B: Yes, I borrowed it from him today.

ab: aicu a inpic su kinisedjam tjai Kuliw?
en: A: Did you borrow this pen from Kuliw?

ab: ini, venli aicu ni kina.
en: B: No, this is what mom bought.

ab: neka anga nu qatiya, sau kisedjamu tjai Umi.
en: A: We’re out of salt, let’s go borrow some from Umi.

ab: uii, vaik aken a kisedjam.
en: B: Okay, I’ll borrow it.

ab: sau qaqivu ti Umi tu pacikel ta kinisedjam a kinpiya tjanuaken.
en: B: Go tell Umi to give me back my money.

ab: pida a kiyam tjanusun?
en: A: How much does she owe you?
